headline: The Skidmarks Leave Their Mark, and Yes, I'm Going to Make That Joke
subheadline: Week 15 gave us a historic beatdown, a tie that shouldn't exist, and standings that make absolutely no sense.
body:
Look, I've been doing this long enough to know that a 10-0 result in fantasy baseball is the sporting equivalent of watching someone get pants'd at prom. It's brutal. It's uncomfortable. Everyone pretends not to look but absolutely everyone is looking.

**Buttville Skidmarks** 10, **General Hospital** 0.

Ten to nothing. A clean sheet. A perfect game of humiliation. This is the fantasy baseball version of the Red Wedding, except instead of the Freys betraying the Starks, it's a team named after underwear stains absolutely annihilating a team named after a soap opera that's been on since 1963. (My mom still watches General Hospital, by the way. She's going to ask me why I'm writing about it and I genuinely don't know what I'm going to tell her.)

The **Skidmarks** put together the kind of week that makes you check if the other guy's team was accidentally on autopilot. Like when you're playing Madden and realize your buddy went to get a sandwich and left his controller on the couch.

Meanwhile, we got ourselves a TIE between the **Riverside Roughnecks** and **Sugar Land Skeeters**. A 5-5 tie. In fantasy baseball. This is like ending a movie right before the climax—imagine The Shawshank Redemption cutting to black the moment Andy starts crawling through the sewage pipe. THAT'S what a fantasy baseball tie feels like.

The **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** (best name in the league, don't @ me, it sounds like something from a Bob's Burgers episode that doesn't exist but absolutely should) demolished **North Correa** 9-1 in what I can only describe as a hate crime against anyone who named their team after Carlos Correa. The Wonderdogs are playing like a team that knows something we don't.

**Canberra Wombats** handled **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** 7-1, which feels significant for reasons I can't fully articulate. Something about Australian marsupials beating up on Midwestern grim reaper imagery just feels cosmically correct in 2024.

Now here's where things get weird: the standings show EVERYONE at 0-0 with zero points for. Every single team. (This is either a glitch in the Matrix or our commissioner fell asleep at the wheel like the guy running the Fyre Festival spreadsheets.)

We're allegedly in Week 15 of the regular season with what appears to be completely broken standings. It's giving major 'Lost' Season 6 energy—we're all just going to pretend this makes sense and hope the ending is satisfying.

Next week: Someone please fix the standings. Also, can the Skidmarks go back-to-back? History says no. My gut says... maybe?
